name: Quebec Apple Dumplings
recipeInstructions:
Make enough pie dough to roll into twelve 6-inch x 6-inch squares (about a double batch).
Sprinkle each square with a spoonful of grated cheese.
Peel and core the apples and place one on each square.
Fill the centre of each apple with a heaping spoonful of mincemeat, a teaspoon of rum, a dot of butter, and 1 tablespoon of sugar.
Bring diagonally opposite corners of the dough over the apple to form ears at the top.
Cover the ears with aluminum foil.
Mix the brown sugar, cream, and grated lemon rind.
Brush the dumplings with this mixture and bake for 35 to 45 minutes in a 400 degree oven.
Remove the foil, brush the ears with the cream mixture, and return to the oven until the ears are brown.
Serve warm with plain cream or sweetened cream, flavored with rum.
